Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{wikidata link|Q11464225}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Otsu-onojinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]]

{{wikidata link|Q11464225}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Otsu-onojinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:moved from a redlink]]

{{wikidata link|Q11464225}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Otsu-onojinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11464225}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Otsu-onojinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]][[Category:Move targets ∪ destinations]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:pages]][[Category:pages]] [[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:pages]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

{{wikidata link|Q11464225}} == Japanese content == {{神社 |名称 = Ono Shrine |画像 = [[Image:Otsu-onojinja.JPG|270px]]
Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]] [[Category:qqqqqqqqqqqqqqqqq]]

Shrine buildings |所在地 = {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}{{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}Ono 1961 |位置 = {{WikidataCoord|display=inline,title}} |祭神 = {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}
{{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} |社格 = {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|lt=major myōjin shrine|1=Myōjin Taisha}})
former {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}} |創建 = Founding era unknown |本殿 = {{ill|lt=shinmei-zukuri|Q930545|WD=Q930545|1=Shinmei-zukuri}} |別名 = |札所 = |例祭 = {{ill|lt=November 2|Q2996|WD=Q2996|1=November 2}} |神事 = Shitogi Festival |地図 = {{Location map|Japan Shiga|label=Ono Shrine|position=bottom|width=200|mark=Shinto torii icon vermillion.svg|marksize=15|float=center|caption=}} {{maplink2|frame=yes|plain=yes|type=point|zoom=9|marker=religious-shinto|frame-align=center|frame-width=200|frame-height=230}} }} '''Ono Shrine''' (おのじんじゃ) is a {{ill|lt=Shinto shrine|Q845945|WD=Q845945|1=Shinto shrine}} in Ono, {{ill|lt=Ōtsu|Q202907|WD=Q202907|1=Ōtsu}}, {{ill|lt=Shiga Prefecture|Q131358|WD=Q131358|1=Shiga Prefecture}}. It is a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}}. Its former rank was {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. The {{ill|lt=enshrined deity|Q11591100|WD=Q11591100|1=Saijin}} is {{ill|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}}, ancestor of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}, and is said to be the ancestral deity of mochi making in Japan. For that reason, the annual Shitogi Festival receives worshipers from confectionery industries across Japan. The shrine crest is Mitsume. === History === This area is the base of the {{ill|lt=Ono clan|Q11464141|WD=Q11464141|1=Ono clan}}. The founding period is unknown, but it was designated a {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Shikinaisha|1=Shikinaisha|WD=Q134917286}} ({{ill|lt=myōjin taisha|Q9610964|WD=Q9610964|1=Myōjin Taisha}}). In {{ill|lt=1891|Q7837|WD=Q7837|1=1891}} ({{ill|lt=Meiji|Q3125096|WD=Q3125096|1=Meiji era}} 24), it was ranked as a {{ill|lt=gōsha|Q134917316|WD=Q134917316|1=Gō-sha}}. === Enshrined deities === * Main deities - {{ill|lt=Ama-tarashihiko-kuni-oshi-hito-no-Mikoto|Q11443593|WD=Q11443593|1=Prince Ametarashihiko}}, {{ill|lt=Tachikara-mochi-tsuki no omi|Q11604425|WD=Q11604425|lt=Kome-mochi-tsuki-ōomi-no-Mikoto|1=Taganetsuki no Ōomi no Mikoto}} === Precinct === [[Image:Onotakamurajinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Takamura Shrine, honden]] [[Image:Onotofujinja.JPG|thumb|Ono no Tōfū Shrine, honden]] * Honden * Chūmon gate * Ono no Takamura Shrine ({{ill|lt=Important Cultural Property|Q1188622|WD=Q1188622|1=Important Cultural Property (Japan)}}) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Takamura|Q1139928|WD=Q1139928|1=Ono no Takamura}}. Built in year 3 of {{ill|lt=Ryakuō|Q1146182|WD=Q1146182|1=Ryakuō}} ({{ill|lt=1340|Q6056|WD=Q6056|1=1340}}). * {{ill|UNKNOWN|lt=Hachiman Shrine|1=Hachiman shrine|WD=Q11390939}} * {{ill|lt=Matsuo Shrine|Q11529677|WD=Q11529677|1=Matsuo shrine}} * Shrine office * Outer precinct shrines ** Ono no Tōfū Shrine (Important Cultural Property) - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Tōfū|Q1439775|WD=Q1439775|1=Ono no Michikaze}}. Built in Ryakuō 4 ({{ill|lt=1341|Q6062|WD=Q6062|1=1341}}). ** Juge Shrine ** {{ill|lt=Yasaka Shrine|Q692714|WD=Q692714|1=Yasaka Shrine}} ** Okamiya Shrine ** Ono no Imoko Shrine - Deity: {{ill|lt=Ono no Imoko|Q1284072|WD=Q1284072|1=Ono no Imoko}}. Built on the mound of Karasu-yama Kofun, said to be Imoko's grave. === Cultural properties === === Important Cultural Properties === * Honden of Ono no Takamura Shrine * Honden of Ono no Tōfū Shrine === Ōtsu City designated tangible cultural property === * One stone hōtō pagoda === Festivals === * November 2 - Shitogi Festival === Notes === {{Reflist}} === External links === *[http://www.shiga-jinjacho.jp/ycBBS/Board.cgi/02_jinja_db/db/ycDB_02jinja-pc-detail.html?mode:view=1&view:oid=117 Ono Shrine] (Shiga Prefectural Shrine Association) *[http://21coe.kokugakuin.ac.jp/db/jinja/210201.html Ono Shrine Two Seats] (Kokugakuin University 21st Century COE Program, Shinto/Shrine Historical Materials Compilation) {{神道 横}} {{Shinto-stub}} == Categories == [[Category:Wikidata generated shikinaisha pages|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Shikinaisha|Ono Shrine (Shiga)]][[Category:Kokuhei-sha]] [[Category:Shikinaisha in Ōmi Province]] [[Category:Shinto shrines in Otsu]] [[Category:Myōjin Taisha]] [[Category:近江国の式内社|名おのしんしや]] [[Category:滋賀県の旧郷社]] [[Category:Cultural heritage monuments in Japan]] [[Category:大津市の重要文化財]] [[Category:Architecture of Muromachi period]] [[Category:小野篁]] [[Category:小野妹子]] [[Category:小野道風]]
